MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Dec-22-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
THE LAW FIRM OF LOW, MCKINLEY BALERIA & SALENKO'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANT AESTHETIC LASER CONCEPTS, INC. is off calendar at the moving parties' request. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Kelley DeYoung, CSR #6780 =(302/RU)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Oct-03-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Jason A. Pollack of Kenneth A. Lipson Law Offices (415.874.3444) appears telephonically as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Christina M. Locher of Low McKinley Baleria & Salenko LLP appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT AESTHETIC LASER CONCEPTS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT and In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues are denied in their entirety. Assuming without deciding that the moving defendant met its burden of production, the motion must still be denied because plaintiff Karen Willis has presented facts that create a triable issue whether Aesthetic Laser Concepts, Inc, is liable as a successor per Ray v. Alad Corp. (1977) 19 Cal.3d 22. Among other things, Ms. Willis has presented evidence showing that the moving defendant operates in the same office as the previous entities and individuals, employs the same nurses as the previous entities and individuals, and uses the same equipment as the previous entities and individuals. Defendant Aesthetic Laser Concept, Inc.'s evidentiary objections are all overruled. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
